detectedObjects: metal bed frame with brass finials

designRecommendations.description: Enhance the existing traditional Indian aesthetic with rich jewel tones, hand-block printed textiles, and carved wooden furniture. Replace the metal stool with a jhoola-inspired side table and add a hand-knotted dhurrie rug. Introduce brass diyas and terracotta wall art to deepen the cultural narrative.
designRecommendations.reasoning: Builds directly on the existing color palette and traditional Indian decor objects already present in the room, requiring mostly textile and accent upgrades.

designRecommendations.description: Simplify the room by replacing the busy bedsheet with a solid mustard or off-white cotton cover and adding a single macrame wall hanging above the bed. Repaint the walls in warm ivory and use the existing curtains as the primary color statement. Add a woven jute rug under the bed for texture.
designRecommendations.reasoning: Reuses existing curtains and furniture while affordable cotton bedding and paint create a fresh cohesive look without major purchases.

designRecommendations.description: Introduce a bold teal accent wall behind the bed to contrast the warm oranges and golds, and replace the stool with a hairpin-leg side table in natural wood. Layer geometric and floral textiles together intentionally — a teal lumbar pillow and a printed throw — to blend contemporary and traditional sensibilities. Install a simple pendant light above the side table for warm ambient glow.
designRecommendations.reasoning: Creates a striking modern-meets-traditional dialogue by introducing a complementary cool teal against the warm existing palette, elevating the room with minimal structural changes.
